description_long: This bungalow features a low-pitched, multi-faceted hip roof with a prominent front porch supported by square columns. The exterior is clad in horizontal siding painted a muted green, complemented by a stone or concrete foundation. Multiple windows with white frames are symmetrically arranged on the front and sides, allowing ample natural light into the interior. The front porch is recessed and covered, providing a sheltered entryway. The structure sits on a rectangular grassy plot, with additional architectural details such as steps leading up to the porch and a modestly elevated base. The overall design is compact and single-storied, typical of early 20th-century American residential architecture.
description: A single-story green bungalow with a hip roof, front porch, and symmetrical white-framed windows, set on a grassy plot.
description_view_0: Rear view showing three symmetrically placed windows and the full expanse of the hip roof.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the depth of the house, a single window, and the side of the porch; also shows some signage or panels on the lawn.
description_view_2: Front view highlighting the covered porch, main entrance, and two large front windows.
description_view_3: Opposite side view displaying a single window and the side profile of the porch, with the hip roof clearly visible.
